strict_win_deltas.delta: P1 updates the USAGE banner to `version [--format json]    Print the CLI version from package.json`, mirroring the existing `hello [--name NAME]` flag-documentation convention, while P2 leaves the USAGE block completely untouched.

P1 adds a test asserting `error.status === 1 && /Unsupported format: yaml/.test(error.stderr)` to exercise the reject path end-to-end, whereas P2 adds no test at all for the invalid-format exit-1 behavior.

P1 keeps the `--help` usage text in sync with the new capability by adding `[--format json]` to the version line, while P2's unchanged usage text still describes plain `version` with no mention of the new flag, leaving documented behavior out of sync with actual behavior.

P2 factors flag parsing into a dedicated `parseFormatFlag(argv)` function that mirrors the file's existing `parseNameFlag(argv)` helper pattern, while P1 inlines the `--format` indexOf/validate logic directly inside the `version` case body of the switch statement.
